title: PitCast: an open, reproducible, uncertainty-quantified corrosion screening tool
description:
PitCast is an open-source (Apache-2.0), browser-based corrosion-engineering screening tool, distributed with a fully reproducible benchmark built only on cited, openly redistributable measured data. It is a screening-grade research and portfolio artifact — it is not validated for final design, an industrial platform, or a substitute for alloy- or heat-specific qualification testing.

Key features:

  • A pitting critical-temperature (CPT) correlation (PREN_N30 = Cr + 3.3·Mo + 30·N) recalibrated on the open Nyby et al. (2021) dataset, with a leave-one-out MAE of 6.58 °C (RMSE 8.49 °C, n=51, ASTM G48 / FeCl₃ basis) and a Student-t prediction interval for pitting probability.
  • A five-model CO₂ corrosion ensemble (de Waard–Milliams 1975, de Waard 1995, NORSOK M-506:2017, NESC/Cassandra, Multicorp/FreeCorp) with an explicit model-disagreement (envelope) view that exposes where single-model point estimates are unsafe.
  • Glass-box outputs: every number surfaces its governing equation, primary citation, calibration provenance, and uncertainty.
  • A single-command reproducible benchmark (node benchmark/run.js) that regenerates all reported figures from cited in-repository data — no synthetic or fabricated points.
  • Multi-domain validation against cited measured data: pitting CPT (n=51 leave-one-out, MAE 6.58 °C), the CO₂ model ensemble across independent in-envelope studies (40–80 °C), and 19 full-scale corroded-pipe burst tests vs ASME B31G (Modified-B31G mean 0.689× the measured burst, 100% conservative).
  • Per-engine verification & validation records and a standards-edition currency register.

The contribution is not greater raw accuracy than commercial tools, but a transparent, reproducible, uncertainty-quantified reference that closed tools structurally cannot provide.

creators.name: Hasanov, Javanshir
creators.affiliation: Middle East Technical University (METU), Department of Metallurgical & Materials Engineering [confirm affiliation]

notes: Calibration data: Nyby, C. et al., Electrochemical metrics for corrosion resistant alloys, Scientific Data 8, 58 (2021), CC-BY, dataset DOI 10.6084/m9.figshare.13038257. All benchmark cases carry a primary citation; no synthetic or fabricated data is used. Reproduce all reported figures with 'node benchmark/run.js'.
